---
applyTo: "{packages/web-platform/web-core/**,packages/web-platform/web-core-e2e/**,packages/react/**/element-template/**,packages/webpack/react-webpack-plugin/**,packages/rspeedy/lynx-bundle-rslib-config/**}"
---

ReactLynx element templates are compile-time assets collected by the React webpack plugin and passed to web-core as `encodeData.elementTemplates`. The web binary encoder may normalize a collected template record into the persisted asset array, but the decoded `ElementTemplates` section and Rust `register_element_templates` path should use only that array shape. For web-core CSR support, emit/register the binary `ElementTemplates` section before `LepusCode`, and include builtins such as `_et_builtin_raw_text` in the asset array rather than synthesizing them in Rust. Keep template DOM instantiation, unique-id registration, class/style updates, CSSOG refreshes, event registration, slot insert/remove, and serialization in Rust/WASM; TypeScript should only route decoded assets and expose the ReactLynx PAPI surface. Use inert HTML `<template>` elements for compiled definitions and invisible anchors for element slots so normal child traversal does not include placeholder nodes.
When building template definitions through custom elements, setting `text` on `x-text` or `raw-text` can run web-elements' `RawTextAttributes` handler and append a text node before the HTML template is cloned. Clear that construction-time side effect in the template definition; the cloned runtime instance should be the only place where the `text` attribute materializes text content.
